meaning: adv.背叛地,靠不住地,危险地
examples: When you say that I murdered him treacherously and foully, you lie.
Marlowe was killed by a man whom he was treacherously trying to stab.
Conditions were likely to become treacherously icy as sub-zero temperatures continued, experts warned.
Until that is over, in February, no candidate is likely even to hint at flexibility towards Turkish-Cypriots for fear of being thought treacherously weak.
